tools.name: send_email
tools.description: Send an email with text or HTML content

tools.name: send_template_email
tools.description: Send an email using a predefined template

tools.name: validate_email
tools.description: Validate email addresses and check deliverability

tools.name: get_email_usage
tools.description: Get email usage statistics — delivery rate, bounce rate, per-domain breakdown, top bounce reasons. Reads from persistent stats.

tools.name: verify_email_setup
tools.description: Verify IP whitelisting and email provider configuration

tools.name: list_email_accounts
tools.description: List all configured IMAP email accounts. Shows default and named accounts (work, personal, etc.).

tools.name: read_inbox
tools.description: Read emails from an inbox via IMAP with filtering options. If IMAP is configured in .env, no credentials needed. Use "account" to pick a named account (work, personal, etc.).

tools.name: get_mailbox_status
tools.description: Get status information for an email mailbox. If IMAP is configured in .env, no credentials needed. Use "account" to pick a named account.

tools.name: list_mailboxes
tools.description: List all available mailboxes for an email account. If IMAP is configured in .env, no credentials needed. Use "account" to pick a named account.

tools.name: start_smtp_receiver
tools.description: Start the SMTP server to receive forwarded corporate emails

tools.name: stop_smtp_receiver
tools.description: Stop the SMTP forward receiver server

tools.name: get_smtp_receiver_status
tools.description: Get the current status of the SMTP forward receiver

tools.name: get_forwarded_emails
tools.description: Retrieve forwarded emails with optional filtering

tools.name: get_conversation_threads
tools.description: Get conversation threads with forwarded emails

tools.name: get_conversation_context
tools.description: Get full conversation context for a specific thread

tools.name: get_threads_for_sender
tools.description: Get all conversation threads involving a specific sender

tools.name: check_email_config
tools.description: Check if email providers are configured, guide setup if needed

tools.name: configure_mailgun
tools.description: Configure Mailgun email provider with step-by-step guidance

tools.name: test_email_config
tools.description: Test email configuration by sending a test email

tools.name: create_mailbox
tools.description: Create a new local mailbox for receiving email (autonomous mode). Each address gets its own inbox accessible via MCP tools.

tools.name: delete_mailbox
tools.description: Delete a local mailbox and all its stored emails (autonomous mode)

tools.name: list_local_mailboxes
tools.description: List all local mailboxes on this server (autonomous mode)

tools.name: request_email_ip
tools.description: Request a dedicated WireGuard IP for email sending. Submits to the provisioning queue.

tools.name: check_ip_status
tools.description: Check the status of a pending WireGuard IP provisioning request

tools.name: get_dns_records
tools.description: Generate all required DNS records (A, MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC) for autonomous email. Outputs copyable records for your DNS provider.

tools.name: verify_autonomous_setup
tools.description: Verify that autonomous email is properly configured: DNS records, DKIM, SMTP send/receive

tools.name: setup_autonomous_security
tools.description: Auto-configure DKIM, SPF, and DMARC for autonomous email. Generates DKIM keys, creates DNS records, and outputs everything needed.

tools.name: create_email_rule
tools.description: Create a rule that triggers actions when incoming emails match conditions. Actions: forward, auto_reply, webhook, flag, move, mcp_tool, log, reject.

tools.name: list_email_rules
tools.description: List all email rules with their match conditions, actions, and hit counts

tools.name: delete_email_rule
tools.description: Delete an email rule by ID

tools.name: update_email_rule
tools.description: Update an existing email rule (enable/disable, change match/actions/priority)

tools.name: start_idle_watch
tools.description: Start real-time IMAP IDLE watching on email accounts. Get push notifications when new emails arrive. Works with Gmail, iCloud, Office365, etc.

tools.name: stop_idle_watch
tools.description: Stop IMAP IDLE watching on an account (or all accounts)

tools.name: get_idle_status
tools.description: Get status of IMAP IDLE watchers — which accounts are being watched, connection status, new email counts

tools.name: provision_start
tools.description: Phase 1: Create a cloud VPS with dedicated IP. Generates WireGuard config. Returns the IP and instructions to connect. Supports DigitalOcean, Vultr, Hetzner, OVH.

tools.name: provision_resume
tools.description: Resume provisioning from where you left off. Automatically detects the current phase and advances: WireGuard connection → DNS setup → TLS → verification. Run this after each user action (connecting WireGuard, adding DNS records).

tools.name: provision_status
tools.description: Show current provisioning state — which phases are complete, what to do next

tools.name: list_cloud_providers
tools.description: List supported cloud providers with pricing, regions, and PTR support info

tools.name: start_health_monitor
tools.description: Start monitoring the provisioned email server — checks tunnel, SMTP, DNS, and IP reputation every 60 seconds. Auto-restarts on failure.

tools.name: get_health_status
tools.description: Get current health status of the email server — tunnel, SMTP, DNS, IP blocklist checks

tools.name: add_sending_domain
tools.description: Add a customer sending domain. Generates DKIM keys and returns the 4 DNS records the customer needs to add. Works like SendGrid domain authentication — self-hosted.

tools.name: list_sending_domains
tools.description: List all configured sending domains with their verification status

tools.name: remove_sending_domain
tools.description: Remove a sending domain and its DKIM keys

tools.name: get_domain_dns_records
tools.description: Get the DNS records a customer needs to add for a sending domain

tools.name: regenerate_zone_file
tools.description: Regenerate the complete DNS zone file for the service domain including all customer subdomains. Use after adding/removing domains.

tools.name: create_email_account
tools.description: Create an email account with SMTP credentials. Returns SMTP password once — save it. The account can then send/receive via this server.

tools.name: delete_email_account
tools.description: Delete an email account and revoke its SMTP credentials

tools.name: list_email_accounts_managed
tools.description: List all email accounts with SMTP credentials (optionally filtered by domain)

tools.name: reset_account_password
tools.description: Reset the SMTP password for an email account. Returns new password once.

tools.name: start_domain_verification
tools.description: Begin a domain-verification window: temporarily set the target domain's MX to verify.setip.io, start (or reuse) the EmailMCP SMTP receiver on :25, and forward any inbound mail for *@domain to forward_to so a human can grab a verification code from the provider (Apple/Google/etc). Auto-tears down aft

tools.name: stop_domain_verification
tools.description: Tear down an active domain-verification window: restore MX (snapshot or supplied real_mx_target), remove the per-domain forwarder. Stops the receiver if no other windows remain.

tools.name: get_domain_verification_status
tools.description: Show active verification windows, the receiver state, and TTLs.

tools.name: get_domain_verification_emails
tools.description: List mails received in the verification window for a given domain (e.g. to find Apple's verification code without checking the forward_to mailbox).

tools.name: set_personal_email_credentials
tools.description: Attach a personal account's own SMTP + IMAP credentials (iCloud, Gmail app password, Fastmail, Office365, etc.) to an existing account. Once set, send_email with from=<address> sends via that SMTP and IMAP-APPENDs a copy to its Sent folder. Passwords are AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest with EMAILMCP_S

tools.name: get_suppression_list
tools.description: View all addresses on the bounce suppression list — these addresses will be rejected on send

tools.name: remove_from_suppression
tools.description: Remove an address from the bounce suppression list (manual override to allow sending again)

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "send_email" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "read_inbox" references sensitive artifacts (.env, credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Read emails from an inbox via IMAP with filtering options. If IMAP is configured in .env, no credentials needed. Use "account" to pick a named account (work, pe…

evidence.snippet: Attach a personal account's own SMTP + IMAP credentials (iCloud, Gmail app password, Fastmail, Office365, etc.) to an existing account. Once set, send_email wit…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: validate_email, get_email_usage, list_email_accounts, read_inbox, get_mailbox_status, list_mailboxes, start_smtp_receiver, get_forwarded_emails, get_conversation_threads, get_conversation_context, get_threads_for_sender, check_email_config, get_dns_records, verify_autonomous_setup, list_email_rules, start_idle_watch, get_idle_status, get_health_status, create_email_account, list_email_accounts_managed, get_domain_verification_emails · Private data: read_inbox, get_mailbox_status, list_mailboxes, get_dns_records, verify_autonomous_setup, setup_autonomous_security, provision_resume, add_sending_domain, get_domain_dns_records, regenerate_zone_file, create_email_account, delete_email_account, list_email_accounts_managed, reset_account_password, set_personal_email_credentials · Public sink: send_email, send_template_email, validate_email, get_email_usage, verify_email_setup, list_email_accounts, get_mailbox_status, list_mailboxes, stop_smtp_receiver, get_smtp_receiver_status, check_email_config, configure_mailgun, test_email_config, create_mailbox, request_email_ip, get_dns_records, verify_autonomous_setup, setup_autonomous_security, create_email_rule, list_email_rules, delete_email_rule, update_email_rule, start_idle_watch, get_idle_status, start_health_monitor, get_health_status, create_email_account, delete_email_account, list_email_accounts_managed, reset_account_password, start_domain_verification, set_personal_email_credentials, get_suppression_list · Destructive: delete_mailbox, delete_email_rule, provision_resume, remove_sending_domain, delete_email_account, reset_account_password, stop_domain_verification, remove_from_suppression.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: validate_email, get_email_usage, list_email_accounts, read_inbox, get_mailbox_status, list_mailboxes, start_smtp_receiver, get_forwarded_emails, get_conversation_threads, get_conversation_context, get_threads_for_sender, check_email_config, get_dns_records, verify_autonomous_setup, list_email_rules, start_idle_watch, get_idle_status, get_health_status, create_email_account, list_email_accounts_managed, get_domain_verification_emails · Private data: read_inbox, get_mailbox_status, list_mailboxes, get_dns_records, verify_autonomous_setup, setup_autonomous_security, provision_resume, add_sending_domain, get_domain_dns_records, regenerate_zone_file, create_email_account, delete_email_account, list_email_accounts_managed, reset_account_password, set_personal_email_credentials · Public sink: send_email, send_template_email, validate_email, get_email_usage, verify_email_setup, list_email_accounts, get_mailbox_status, list_mailboxes, stop_smtp_receiver, get_smtp_receiver_status, check_email_config, configure_mailgun, test_email_config, create_mailbox, request_email_ip, get_dns_records, verify_autonomous_setup, setup_autonomous_security, create_email_rule, list_email_rules, delete_email_rule, update_email_rule, start_idle_watch, get_idle_status, start_health_monitor, get_health_status, create_email_account, delete_email_account, list_email_accounts_managed, reset_account_password, start_domain_verification, set_personal_email_credentials, get_suppression_list · Destructive: delete_mailbox, delete_email_rule, provision_resume, remove_sending_domain, delete_email_account, reset_account_password, stop_domain_verification, remove_from_suppression

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
